task_completion.description: Create the basic geometry of two crossed arms.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The main canvas shows two line segments intersecting each other to form an 'X' shape.

task_completion.description: Constrain the bottom endpoints to the origin and X-axis.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The bottom-left endpoint of the 'X' is snapped to the origin (intersection of the red and green axes). The bottom-right endpoint lies exactly on the horizontal (X) axis.

task_completion.description: Dimension the horizontal base width to 100.0 mm.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A horizontal distance dimension of '100' or '100.00' is visible between the bottom-left and bottom-right endpoints.

task_completion.description: Dimension the arms to a total length of 260.0 mm.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Length dimensions of '260' or '260.00' are visible on both full arms, OR dimensions of '130' or '130.00' are visible on the half-segments from the pivot.

task_completion.description: Constrain the arms to pivot exactly at their midpoints.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The intersection of the two lines is perfectly symmetric, indicating a midpoint constraint. This may be evidenced by equal length constraints, '130' dimensions on all four halves, or a midpoint constraint listed in the property browser.

task_completion.description: Ensure the sketch is fully constrained (0 DOF) and saved correctly.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text window/property browser explicitly states '0 degrees of freedom' (or 'ok'). The window title bar or a save dialog shows the filename 'scissor_lift.slvs'.

integrity.description: The agent used the SolveSpace GUI to build the model.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the SolveSpace graphical interface, toolbars, and canvas, rather than just a text editor writing a .slvs file.

integrity.description: The agent did not explicitly dimension the height to cheat the solver.
integrity.visual_evidence: There should be NO driving vertical dimension of '240' or '240.00' forcing the top points. If 240 is visible, it must clearly be a reference/driven measurement, not a driving constraint.

integrity.description: The geometric proportions match the mathematical reality.
integrity.visual_evidence: The drawn 'X' shape should visually appear much taller than it is wide (240mm height vs 100mm width), confirming the solver actually computed the kinematics.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected base width is 100.0 mm. The expected full arm length is 260.0 mm (or 130.0 mm per half-arm). The resulting extended height must be exactly 240.0 mm. The sketch must be fully constrained (0 DOF) without explicitly dimensioning the height. The final file must be saved as scissor_lift.slvs.
